user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a vocabulary base and feedback method when providing a low level document path, so that I can customize how the system processes my input.
true_user_story: The START NEW PROJECT function shall allow the user to create a new project.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to configure IR Method and Vocabulary Base options during project setup, so that I can tailor the retrieval process to my needs before creating the project.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a user, I want to optionally select a weighting method and vocabulary base when providing a high level document, so that I can customize how the document is processed and compared.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be required to select a Weight Threshold when choosing Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF as the IR Method, so that the system can properly filter keywords based on importance.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide both high level and low level document paths, and optionally select a feedback method, so that the system can process my documents with my preferred feedback approach.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be required to select a weight threshold when choosing Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF, so that the system can properly configure the IR method.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide a Stop Word file and a new project name, and to select a Weight Threshold when using Keyword Extraction methods, so that my project setup is complete and tailored to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide Weight Option from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to start a new project by providing a low level document path and have the option to cancel at any time before creation, so that I have control and flexibility during project setup.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF, the user must also select a Weight Threshold.

user_story: As a user, I want to select a Weight Threshold when choosing Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF as the IR Method, so that I can control the relevance of extracted keywords.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Thesaurus Filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide a high level document and optionally select weighting and vocabulary options, so that I can customize how my documents are processed.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the Stop Word file.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted for necessary project details and optional Stop Word file when creating a new project, so that I can set up my project in Trace mode efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Feedback Method.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a Weight Threshold when choosing Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF as the IR Method, so that I can configure the project appropriately.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a user, I want to create a new project by providing a name and optionally selecting an IR Method and Vocabulary Base, so that I can customize my project setup according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in “Trace” mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to select a project and a feedback method before loading, so that I can work on the correct project with the desired feedback approach.
true_user_story: Since the majority of projects are not completed in a single setting, then it is critical to provide the user the option to load an existing project to continue working on the project.  Load Project feature will provide the user the capacity to open an existing project in the last saved state with all the associated mode options. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to load an existing project and select the feedback method, so that I can continue working from where I left off and use my preferred feedback options.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a project name from a list of saved projects.  Project names are obtained by scanning the saved projects directory.

user_story: As a user, I want to select and load an existing project from a list of saved projects, so that I can continue working from where I left off with all my previous settings.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a feedback method.  The only option is “Standard Rochio”.

user_story: As a user, I want to select a project and a feedback method before loading, so that I can work on the correct project with the desired feedback approach.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the LOAD PROJECT action and return to the previous activity.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an existing project from a list of saved projects, so that I can continue working from where I left off.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load the project once the project name and feedback method is selected.

user_story: As a user, I want to select a weight threshold when choosing Keyword Extraction methods, so that I can control the relevance of extracted keywords.
true_user_story: Similar in functionality to the START NEW PROJECT function, the LOAD RTM function collects project parameters from the user and initializes the project.  A project created via the LOAD RTM function may be in either “trace” or “assess” mode.  Assess mode is usually used when the user is provided a RTM and needs to evaluate its accuracy, completeness, and satisfaction level.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the RTM file path, select a weight option, and choose a feedback method, so that I can customize the retrieval process according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a user, I want to review and modify my selected options before creating the project, so that I can ensure all settings are correct.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want to create a new project by providing a name and, if needed, a weight threshold, so that the project is set up correctly in the desired mode.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a project mode, provide a low level document path, and optionally specify a thesaurus file, so that I can configure my project setup efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the RTM file path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to enter a new project name, provide a high level document path, and optionally select a Vocabulary Base, so that I can set up my project with the correct configuration.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to select the project mode and provide a low level document path, so that I can configure my project setup according to my needs before creation.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the paths for both low and high level documents, and optionally a stop word file, so that I can configure the document processing as needed.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide “Weight Option” from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user, I want to load an RTM and select its mode and weight option, so that I can assess or trace requirements with appropriate parameters.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of “Keyword Extraction” or “Keyword Extraction-IDF”, the user must also select a “Weight Threshold”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be required to select a Weight Threshold when choosing Keyword Extraction methods, so that the system can properly filter keywords based on importance.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Thesaurus” filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to configure retrieval settings such as thesaurus file, IR method, and weight option, so that I can customize my search experience.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Stop Word” filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted for the new project name, RTM file path, and optionally select a Vocabulary Base, so that I can configure my project setup efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Feedback Method”.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the low level document path, so that I can include all necessary documents for project creation.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Project Mode” of either “Trace” or “Assess”.  “Trace” is the default mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to optionally specify a feedback method and stop word file when loading an RTM project, so that I can customize the project initialization according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an RTM and choose its mode (trace or assess), so that I can either work with or evaluate the RTM's accuracy and completeness.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in specified “Project Mode”.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to automatically save all my current data and project when activated, so that I don't lose any progress.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to activate the SAVE function to save the current data and status of the project.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to save the current data and status of my project, so that I can preserve my progress and avoid losing important information.
true_user_story: When activated RETRO will save all current data and project.

user_story: As a user, I want to close the current project, so that I can finish my work and prevent further changes.
true_user_story: The CLOSE CURRENT PROJECT Function will allow the user to close a given project without exiting the RETRO application.

user_story: As a user, I want to close the current project without exiting the RETRO application, so that I can switch between projects efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be able close the current project.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to close the application easily, so that I can end my session whenever I choose.
true_user_story: It is necessary to provide a mechanism to exit the RETRO application when the user is finished working with the application.

user_story: As a user, I want an option to exit the RETRO application, so that I can close it easily when I am done working.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to exit the application.

user_story: As a user, I want to download the RTM in XML format, so that I can use it in other tools or for offline analysis.
true_user_story: The user may want to view the current project’s XML file format.  The SHOW XML function will provide the user a display of the current XML file.

user_story: As a user, I want to view the current project's XML file, so that I can inspect its structure and contents directly.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to view the XML version of the RTM.

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save the “Requirements with no associated tests report”, so that I can identify requirements that lack test coverage.
true_user_story: After a project has completed either a trace or an assess process, then the user will need the capability to review the final results of the process in an easy to read format other than the XML file format that is automatically generated throughout the process.  The GENERATE REPORT function will provide the user a list of reports that the user may view.  The user will have the option to save the generated report or modify the design of the report. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and view various process reports in a readable format, so that I can easily review and save the results of completed trace or assess processes.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “RTM report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate, view, and save various project reports in a readable format, so that I can easily review and share the results of trace or assess processes.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parent with no children report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save the “Children with parents report”, so that I can keep records of children who have parents.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parents with no children with text report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save the “Children with no parents report”, so that I can identify orphaned items in the system.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and view various process reports in a readable format, so that I can easily review results and save or customize the reports as needed.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents with text report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save various text-based reports, so that I can analyze and document relationships efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save the "Requirements Traceability Matrix (RTM) report," so that I can track the coverage and status of requirements efficiently for project documentation and audits.  This helps ensure all requirements are accounted for and traceable throughout the project lifecycle.  This feature should be consistent with other report generation functionalities, such as the "Parent with no children report" and the "Not satisfied requirements report."  This will aid, so that I
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements with text report”

user_story: As a user, I want to filter and display only the top N low-level requirements above a specified weight threshold, so that I can focus on the most relevant elements and remove unnecessary ones.
true_user_story: “The user shall be able to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to filter and display only the top N requirements, so that I can focus on the most relevant elements and manage hidden links efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate whether the filter process applies to the selected high level document element (LOCAL) or to all high level document elements (GLOBAL).

user_story: As a user, I want to filter requirement links locally or globally, so that I can control the scope of displayed requirements for better focus and management.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to filter the list of low-level requirements by setting a cutoff point for the weight between 0 and 1.0.

user_story: As a user, I want to filter and display only relevant requirement links at either a local or global level, so that I can focus on the most pertinent information and manage document complexity efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate that the top N elements to be displayed.

user_story: As a user, I want to filter low-level requirements by weight and choose whether the filter applies locally or globally, so that I can efficiently review relevant requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able activate a KILL HIDDEN Links function to remove all elements not currently displayed.

user_story: As a user, I want to sort low level documents by different attributes, so that I can easily organize and find the information I need.
true_user_story: The user will need the ability to view the text of low-level requirements.  The DISPLAY function will provide three different sort options to view the text of low-level requirements.  The user can sort the low-level requirements based upon the weight of linking relationships, original document order, or by requirement id. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to sort low-level requirements by requirement ID, so that I can easily locate and review specific requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Requirement ID”

user_story: As a user, I want to sort low-level requirements by document order, requirement ID, or relationship weight, so that I can efficiently view and organize requirement texts.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Weights”

user_story: As a user, I want to sort low-level requirements by weight, requirement ID, or original document order, so that I can easily view and organize the requirements according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Document Order”

user_story: As a user, I want to select a low level requirement and view its link status, so that I can easily determine if it is linked, not linked, or set to default.
true_user_story: Since RETRO’s tracing and assessing modes are based upon algorithms that will allow processes to be improved as the system receives FEEDBACK from the user.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to adapt its tracing and assessing processes based on my feedback, so that the system continuously improves and better meets my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall select a low level requirement and identify the link status from the following list: Link No Link Default

user_story: As a user, I want to switch between multiple open projects in the RETRO System, so that I can efficiently manage and compare my work across different projects.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load multiple projects at the same time.

user_story: As a user, I want to switch between multiple open projects easily, so that I can work on several tasks without closing and reopening projects.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall allow multiple projects to be open at the same time

user_story: As a user, I want to convert my existing input text files into the RETRO file format, so that I can use my current data with the RETRO System.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to import projects.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to import existing projects into the system, so that I can quickly set up and manage ongoing work without starting from scratch.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a mechanism to convert existing input text files into RETRO file format.  (Not implemented.)

user_story: As a user, I want a graphical user interface to interact with the RETRO System, so that I can easily access and use its features.
true_user_story: The user shall use a GUI INTERFACE to work with RETRO.

user_story: As a user, I want to interact with RETRO through a graphical user interface, so that I can easily access and use its features without needing command-line knowledge.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a GUI mechanism for the user to interact with the RETRO System.

user_story: As a user, I want to enter a keyword into a search box to find matches within both high-level and low-level requirements, so that I can quickly locate relevant information.
true_user_story: The user is provided the capability via BROWSE mode to manually link low-level requirements to high-level requirement. In order to help the user to quickly locate the desired low-level requirement or high-level requirement, a KEYWORD SEARCH function is offered to the user. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to search for high-level requirements using keywords, so that I can quickly find and link them to low-level requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within low-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a user, I want to search for high-level requirements using keywords, so that I can quickly find and link them to low-level requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within high-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to link and unlink low-level requirements to high-level requirements, so that I can maintain clear traceability between different requirement levels.
true_user_story: There will be times when the user will not agree with RETRO’s list of candidate links.  Therefore, it is important to provide a mechanism for the user to manually change, create, or delete links between low and high level requirements.  BROWSE mode is the mechanism within RETRO that enables the user to perform MANUAL LINKING/TRACING. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to manually create, modify, or delete links between requirements in BROWSE mode, so that I can ensure the traceability accurately reflects my intent.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create and remove low-level requirements with high-level requirements.

user_story: As a user, I want to generate traces for selected requirements, so that I can track their relationships and ensure coverage.
true_user_story: One of the purposes of RETRO is to aid the user in linking low-level requirements with high-level requirements.  TRACING functionality is the component of RETRO that automatically generates a list of candidate links of high to low-level requirements.

user_story: As a user, I want to automatically generate candidate trace links between high-level and low-level requirements, so that I can efficiently establish traceability in my project.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on all requirements.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to automatically suggest links between high-level and low-level requirements, so that I can efficiently trace and validate requirement relationships.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on a given requirement(s).

user_story: As a user, I want to filter and view candidate links based on tracing and permit feedback, so that I can efficiently identify relevant connections.
true_user_story: After the TRACING function is performed, then the results of the TRACING process will need to be made available to the user to view.  The TRACING RESULTS functionality will display a list of all candidate links of high to low-level requirements based on any given filtering criteria.  The user will be allowed to provide feedback on the TRACING RESULTS in order to improve future TRACING.

user_story: As a user, I want to view and filter the TRACING RESULTS after running the TRACING function, so that I can review candidate requirement links and provide feedback to improve future TRACING accuracy.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display the list of candidate links based on the tracing and permit feedback, filtering, and display functionality.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to save the current status of my trace, so that I can resume my work later without losing progress.
true_user_story: The SAVE TRACE function shall allow the user to save the trace result.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to save the trace results, so that I can review or share them later.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism to save the current status of trace.

user_story: As a project manager, I want RETRO to lock completed projects, so that no further changes can be made after completion.
true_user_story: The COMPLETING TRACE function shall allow user to finish the tracing process.

user_story: As a user, I want to complete the tracing process, so that I can finalize and save my work efficiently.
true_user_story: RETRO shall mark the project completed and not permit further changes.

user_story: As a user, I want to run an accuracy and completeness check by tracing requirements and comparing IR method results to the RTM, so that I can verify the correctness of the requirements traceability.
true_user_story: The user needs a mechanism that will help to determine whether the TRACING was successfully performed based on accuracy and completeness.  An accuracy test tells the user that the candidate links provided are correctly related to the high-level requirement.  Completeness test tells the user that all possible links that are listed in the candidate list are included and no low-level requirements are missing. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want a tool to evaluate the accuracy and completeness of tracing candidate links, so that I can ensure all high-level requirements are correctly and fully traced to low-level requirements.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to be able to run an ACCURACY & COMPLETENESS check by performing a full trace on the requirements and comparing the IR method results to the given RTM. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to show or hide recommendations, so that I can control what information is displayed to me.
true_user_story: “The [user] will need to have a list of recommendations based on RETRO’s TRACING process.  SHOW RECOMMENDATION will display to the [user] a list of recommendation while in ASSESS mode.  The [user] will have the capability to provide FEEDBACK to the RETRO system on whether or not the [user] agrees with RETRO’s assessment of the linking between the low-level requirements to the high-level requirements.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to view a list of recommendations generated by RETRO’s TRACING process in ASSESS mode, so that I can provide feedback on the accuracy of the requirement linkages.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display/hide recommendations at the user’s request.

user_story: As a user, I want to see the percentage level of link displayed, so that I can easily understand the current link status.
true_user_story: Satisfaction function shows how satisfied the high-level requirement is with the given set of low-level requirements. [4] The Satisfaction Analysis function is checking the level of percentage for a satisfaction of the linked result with the high and low level input files. Once final output is agreed, the list of high level requirement is updated with options like “Satisfied”, “Not Satisfied” and “Partially Satisfied”. The status of high level requirement is color coded. Unsatisfied result can be repeated until all the high levels has been satisfied stage.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to see the satisfaction status of high-level requirements based on linked low-level requirements, so that I can easily track which requirements are satisfied, partially satisfied, or not satisfied and take appropriate actions.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display to the user the percentage level of link. [1]

user_story: As a content manager, I want RETRO to automatically remove all links marked as “No Link”, so that the content remains accurate and free of broken or irrelevant links.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism to allow the removal of all low-level requirements that are not linked to a high-level requirement.  REMOVE ALL NO LINKS function will provide an easy way for the user to quickly remove low-level requirements with status of “No Link”.

user_story: As a user in ASSESS mode, I want a REMOVE ALL NO LINKS function to quickly delete all low-level requirements not linked to high-level requirements, so that I can efficiently clean up unlinked items.
true_user_story: RETRO shall remove all links marked as “No Link”

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to delete a recommended link from my list, so that I can manage and remove suggestions I no longer need.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism that will allow the deletion of recommended links that the user feels are not correctly linked.

user_story: As a user in ASSESS mode, I want to be able to delete recommended links that are incorrect, so that my workspace only contains relevant and accurate connections.
true_user_story: RETRO shall permit the user to select a recommended link and delete it.

user_story: As a user, I want each candidate link to be color-coded based on its satisfaction status, so that I can quickly identify which links meet the criteria.
true_user_story: “In order to help the user to quickly identify the level of satisfaction of each candidate link, RETRO will need to provide a visual aid.  COLOR-CODED SATISFACTION STATUS function will color code each candidate link based on its satisfaction level.   The various colors will be a visual aid to the user to help quickly identify each links satisfaction level.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want candidate links to be color-coded by satisfaction status, so that I can quickly identify how satisfied each link is at a glance.
true_user_story: RETRO shall COLOR-CODE THE SATISFACTION STATUS of each given candidate link.
